candidates.stmt: Liam McQuade has lived in the ward for over twenty five years. He is standing so that the voters of Bethnal Green East will have the opportunity to show their support for a national party which has taken a strong stand against climate change, racism and genocide and is committed to a more equal society.
Chelsea Omoogun has lived in Bethnal Green West since birth, and aims to boost political participation as well as campaigning about the inclusion of more youth spaces in the area.

**Standing for our community, not a political party, in the election on 7 May**

I am a dad of four, a project manager and community organiser. I’ve spoken to 1,000s of people in the ward, knocking on doors, listening to their concerns and challenges. I am the only independent candidate to be a councillor in Bethnal Green West. A vote for me is a vote for change, a vote for a better way of doing politics in Tower Hamlets.

**Whoever the mayor is**, if I become your independent councillor, I will:
- Represent EVERYONE in Bethnal Green West - I’ll be your voice and get you answers
- Work for safer streets throughout the area
- Pressure landlords to improve housing management
- Work with charities and schools to improve youth knowledge and skills for the job market
- Protect our community spaces like Canal Club Community Centre, Poyser Street outdoor seating and the Bethnal Green Nature Reserve
- Show people what a good councillor looks like

_This is not a party manifesto, but a personal commitment by the only independent candidate for Bethnal Green West._

I live in Bethnal Green West with my family, and I’m proud to call this community home.
I’m a visiting researcher at Middlesex University, focusing on how local politics and democracy can be strengthened to counter the rise of the far right.

I also serve as a co-op governor at Lawdale Junior School and was a Councillor in Hackney between 2006 and 2014, which taught me how to listen, solve problems and get things done. I’m committed to safer streets for women and girls and I’m part of a grassroots movement with parents promoting smartphone-free childhood.

I’m standing up for Bethnal Green West to make sure our communities’ interests are heard.

I am standing as an independent candidate to be one of the three Councillors for Blackwall & Cubitt Town ward in Tower Hamlets
I believe based on the 2022 local election results that I am the only candidate who can beat the 3 Aspire Councillor candidates and ensure that there is some opposition left in Tower Hamlets as my concern this May is to avoid an Aspire landslide win which would be bad for all of us including even Aspire

How am I qualified to be one of your three Councillors? 
Do an an internet search for ’andrew wood tower hamlets’ to find out more about me
Experience – I was the Councillor for the neighbouring Canary Wharf ward from 2014 to 2022, but I did a lot of work in Blackwall & Cubitt Town ward as well then
Since 2023 I have worked in senior finance roles for two other London Councils
I am a qualified accountant (CIMA), former secondary school governor and former Chair of the Royal London Hospital patient panel
I have lived on Westferry Road on the island since 2009
Hard work – the only thing I can guarantee is that I will work hard on your behalf, see my social media for evidence of that 
Knowledge – I know a lot about the area, local politics and the problems we face 
Passion – I do this because I am passionate about better government for this area
Because I can win – this May the most likely election outcome is an Aspire landslide, that won’t be good for everybody including Aspire, I am the only person who can beat Aspire for at least one of the three Councilors positions

What will I do if elected as one of your three Councillor's
A reminder that in Tower Hamlets, only the Executive Mayor has power due to the previous referendum votes
The main job of Councillor's is to scrutinize the Mayor, sit on Committees, and to represent their residents
BUT there are things that Councillor's can do if they push hard which my next leaflet will illustrate but here are a few examples:
Restart the Safer Neighbourwood ward Panel so that residents, the Council an

Dear Residents,
I am pleased to introduce myself as a Reform Party candidate.
I am seeking to represent you as a councillor for Blackwall and Cubitt Town.
Many of you are likely familiar with the Reform Party’s national policies. I would like to share my local priorities with you.
I have worked as a live-in carer for over 10 years, supporting clients in their homes.
My responsibilities included daily care, preparing meals, and providing companionship.
I am proud of my work as a carer and want to see improved care for the elderly and vulnerable.
Carers deserve recognition, better pay, and improved working conditions.
This change can begin with the council, which oversees assessing, arranging, and funding social care.
Resident safety is a top priority. Many have expressed the need for a greater police presence.
If elected, I will work with fellow councillors to reinstate the Safer Neighbourhood ward panel.
Making our streets and communities safer must be a council priority.
Mudchute Park and Farm is a valued community resource.
The recent rent increase to £15,000 per year means the farm needs more community support.
The council previously provided an annual grant to support the farm’s operations.
The farm has served the community since 1977 and should remain in Tower Hamlets for future generations.

Houseboaters face being priced off the canals, and I am here to bring your concerns to the Canal and River Trust.
Although I am new to politics, my common-sense approach and independence may help me find solutions others overlook.
If you are seeking a strong advocate, I am ready to be your voice. I will work hard and remain accessible if elected.
I enjoy working with people and will always listen to your concerns.
Yours sincerely
Catherine Prescott

Richard Flowers is an activist and Lib Dem campaigner in East London, where he has lived for 25 years. Passionate about London's diversity and liberal values, he wants to build prosperity and tackle poverty and pollution in the East End, stop Brexit and stand up for his values of freedom, fairness and the future.

Originally from the North West, in 2017 he stood for Parliament for the Liberal Democrats in Macclesfield, near to his old home town.

Richard puts his values into action as Treasurer of the Party’s diversity campaign LGBT+ Liberal Democrats, the Humanist and Secularist Liberal Democrats, and the pro-migration pressure group Lib Dem Immigrants.

Richard lives with his husband Alex. They were able to get married thanks to Liberal Democrats in government creating the Same-Sex Marriage Act.

His proudest achievements are contributing to that Same-Sex Marriage campaign and, as Chair of Tower Hamlets Lib Dems in 2016, being part of a team that got a 69% vote for Remain in the Referendum.

Jonathan Purcell lives in Bow and works in the charity sector. Before his current work on justice for Palestinians, he worked in environmentalism, disability policy and community relations. Bow has a proud local tradition of being a place for everyone, and social justice and transparency must be at the heart of the councillors that represent us.

MANSUR NADIM HUSSAIN

▪ Maths teacher at Southwark PRU
▪ Born and raised in Tower Hamlets and actively engages with the young community
▪ Passionate about supporting local residents
Rupert George lives in Bow West. He has two children at Chisenhale school. He is concerned about and campaigns on road safety and the cleanliness of public spaces.
Martin Parker, who has lived in the area since the 80’s, campaigns for good quality and more affordable housing. As well as provision of more green and open spaces.

Momtaz Islam is a housing and litigation solicitor, founder of Access to Advocacy and SHE — Supporting Her Empowerment. She grew up in Tower Hamlets and is standing for Bow West to give back to the community that shaped her. She campaigns for genuinely affordable housing, transparent governance, safer streets, free legal support for residents and real opportunities for young people. She believes in a diverse Tower Hamlets where every resident — regardless of background, faith or ethnicity — feels heard, represented and valued.

Samia Hersi is the Conservative Prospective Parliamentary Candidate for Queen's Park and Maida Vale. 

Samia is a born and raised Londoner. She was born in Edgware in North London and grew up in Bow in East London. Samia is the daughter of Somali immigrants. Her father was a refugee who fled the civil war in Somalia. He was a bus driver based at Cricklewood Bus Garage on Edgware Road and her mother was a cleaner. 

From a young age, Samia's parents instilled in me the values of hard work and aspiration to achieve a better life for herself and others.

After she graduated from University, Samia worked in schools across London for an education charity, IntoUniversity, and its partner charity, The Clement James Centre, based in North Kensington. She was supporting children and young people in schools across Queen's Park and Maida Vale, including at Paddington Academy, to aspire and achieve. 

Samia previously worked as a Parliamentary Researcher for Rt Hon. Gillian Keegan, Member of Parliament for Chichester. She is now a policy and public affairs adviser for a communications agency advising FTSE and NASDAQ 100 companies on financial services and technology policy.

Alistair has lived in Tower Hamlets since 1991. He is a governor of a local school and has been involved in many local campaigns. Alistair is a criminal lawyer by trade. Alistair joined the Green Party 15 years ago, because he believes it brings people together to strengthen local communities and focus our attention on the climate crises. In a world of division, Greens believe in working to unite our communities and creating hope.

My name is Mohamed Aboshanab, known locally as Mo. I’m standing as an independent candidate for Canary Wharf because I believe this area deserves practical leadership, not party politics.

I live locally and work with residents, entrepreneurs, and families across Canary Wharf and Tower Hamlets every week. Through my work and community engagement, I hear the same concerns again and again: rising service charges, poor accountability from developers and managing agents, safety and transport issues, lack of real engagement from councillors, and decisions being made without residents’ voices.

I am standing as an independent because I do not answer to party whips, donors, or national agendas. I answer only to residents.

My priorities are simple and focused:

Strong accountability for developers, estate managers, and housing providers

Fair service charges and transparency for leaseholders and renters

Safer streets, better lighting, and visible local policing

Protecting local businesses and supporting independent traders

Ensuring Canary Wharf residents have a real voice at Tower Hamlets Council

Practical solutions, not ideology

I believe local government should be about listening, solving problems, and delivering results. Canary Wharf is one of London’s most important economic hubs, yet too often residents feel ignored. That needs to change.

If elected, I commit to being accessible, visible, and accountable. You will see me on the streets, at meetings, and challenging decisions that do not serve our community.

This campaign is about residents first, always.

Paul has lived in Bethnal Green for the last 21 years, but traces his family history in the neighbourhood back to 19th-century Jewish migrants. A Green Party member for 16 years, he has been involved in environmental activism since he was a teenager, and now works in several voluntary roles to promote greener practices in the performing arts sector. Alongside his work as a theatre designer, he is artistic director at a queer-led theatre company and works with Muslim and Jewish creatives to run cross-cultural community storytelling and music events in the borough.

After the last 14 years we have seen austerity, war and poverty under this rotten Tory government.   

We have seen the horrific response to COVID that the Tories delivered, from giving out dodgy PPE contacts, to underfunding and privatizing the NHS causing thousands to die in addition to this the tories partied throughout it all.    

We have seen the impact of austerity first hand with infrastructure crumbling, bus routes cut, NHS resources stretched, youth services closed and so much more. The implementation of austerity measures has seen many lives made harder or even lost, for example cutting services to the NHS meant more people died of covid.   There's also the horrific events of war happening not only in Ukraine and Gaza but also in Sudan, Yemen, Myanmar and all over the world. Where innocent people are bombed so that some governments can expand their borders a few meters.  

 In Gaza after the horrific attacks on October 7th, we've seen the horrendous war crimes committed by the Israeli state against the people of Gaza, and the west's horrific response backing the Israeli states war crimes to the hilt. At least over 36,550 people have been killed in Gaza including at least 15,000 children and more than 82,959 people injured & more than 10,000 missing under the rubble. People are starving, thirsty, homeless, whilst also having to fear the bombs dropping over them.   

What's Labours Starmers response to the war crimes in Gaza? Starmer has come out and said its okay “to cut food, water and electricity off the people of Gaza” This is meant to be the leader of the Labour Party a party that was once the party of the working class, now it's the party of big business, austerity, war and capitalism. Taking ages to come out in favour of a “ceasefire” even then Labours version of a “ceasefire” was only called by labour due to the pressure of the anti war movement where hundreds of thousands were on the streets in London and millions globally against israeli state ter

A City professional and proud Limehouse resident, Jurgen is committed to our community. He is campaigning for stronger tenants’ rights and the creation of welcoming third spaces where neighbours can connect. Jurgen is dedicated to making our streets and parks safer. He is looking to build a strong sense of community cohesion. He believes everyone should be able to walk in their street and feel safe, happy, and energised.

**Jacob Sinkins** is a political researcher and former charity worker, who moved to East London from Norfolk in 2023. An experienced campaigner, with a record of delivering for communities, Jacob is ready to step up to deliver on the priorities of Limehouse locals.

As a young professional, he understands the difficulties of the housing market and the soaring cost of living. He is also determined to work with Liberal Democrat colleagues to secure stronger rights and protections for leaseholders, who have been let down by Conservative and Labour Governments.

Jacob is a keen runner, who enjoyed passing through Limehouse while completing the 2025 London Marathon. He also enjoys live music, and following his beloved Norwich City around the country.

Martin has lived in Mile End since 2002. He currently works in the tech industry and can often be seen cycling between his home in Mile End and data centres in the borough. Previously, he worked at Queen Mary University of London, looking after the welfare and immigration status of international students. He’s an active member of the local community and has been a beekeeper in Tower Hamlets Cemetery Park for many years. Recently, as a result of Eastend Homes’ disastrous project to add floors to buildings on the Mile End Estate, Martin has become a housing campaigner. He chairs the Coniston House Tenants and Residents Association and has appeared on TV and at the Mayor of London and London Assembly’s People’s Question Time, advocating on behalf of him and his neighbours.

His name truly reflects his character. Amin Rahman.
Alongside his roles as a school governor and a college lecturer, Amin remains committed to public service. He is ready to take on new responsibilities and serve the community with honesty, integrity, respect and care.
He is a retired Wing Commander equivalent to a Lieutenant Colonel from the Bangladesh Air Force and later worked as an airline pilot and in senior management roles.
Amin would be honoured to have your support.

Hytham Emam has lived in Tower Hamlets for eight years and is deeply committed to the borough’s future. A Civil Engineer with an MBA and over 20 years of experience in renewable energy and infrastructure, he is now a tech entrepreneur focused on cleaner transport & mobility. He is standing for the Green Party because he believes professional expertise should be used to solve the everyday challenges facing our community specifically: 
1 - Improving new skills within youth and non-employed to get into new technologies and industries.
2 - Putting rent controls, reducing service charges and fixing the housing repair backlog.
3 - Making our streets and parks safer, cleaner and healthier for children.

Hytham Emam is passionate about using modern technology to make council services more transparent and responsive to residents’ needs. Beyond his professional life, he is an avid hiker and world traveller who values the global perspective that comes from exploring different cultures. He wants to bring that same energy and problem-solving mindset to the Town Hall, ensuring Mile End is a safe, sustainable, and forward-looking home where every resident can thrive.

The following are my personal passion policies;

1. Igniting Ambition: From the classroom to the boardroom

2. Anchoring Our Families: Ending 'out-of-borough' housing displacement

3. Reclaiming Our Green Spaces: A clean and safe Mile End park

4. Radical Representation: A council that mirrors all it's people

Bethan has been a member of the Green Party for a number of years and has lived and worked in Tower Hamlets for over 15 years. A Law graduate with an MA in Human Rights, Bethan is committed to social justice and has a particular interest in migration and women's issues.

Bethan has served on the management committees of London Citizens, a community organising organisation working for the common good, and of the Jesuit Refugee Service. She has campaigned around issues of undocumented migrants, affordable housing and the living wage. In 2016 she worked with a team of women to set up an exhibition of East End women's history in response to the opening of the controversial Jack the Ripper "museum".

Bethan has two school-age children. She enjoys reading, running, local history and being involved in the local church.

Moving to the area from London in 2018, Steph previously worked with vulnerable people as a housing officer before following her passion for food and the environment.

She has spent the last few years volunteering on a number of food and farming-related projects and founded the Incredible Edible group in 2020; installing a veg patch in Leigh Library Gardens, running volunteer sessions and guerrilla gardening on unloved patches of land.

She would love to make Southend greener, encouraging pride in our area and a connection to nature for all whilst tackling the biodiversity and climate crisis. She currently runs a school gardening club and is passionate about encouraging children to grow food.

Steph is a committed environmental activist with a degree in Politics and Media and is currently studying for an MSc in Sustainable Food at the Centre for Alternative Technology.

Roddy lives in Stepney Green and works nearby for a non-profit organisation that works to expand internet access in low- and middle-income countries. As a Green Councillor, he will fight to secure long-term leases for charities and cultural institutions in our area – giving them the stability they need to plan for the future and continue enriching our community.

Phoebe Gill has lived in Whitechapel since 2019 and works nearby. Once elected, she will push for local residents’ priorities, including cleaner and safer streets. She supports our community’s proud tradition of anti-racism and standing up to the far right

candidates.n: Shah Muhammad Rezaul Islam Suhel Ameen
